verdict_changed_after_codex_review.reason: Codex adversarial review of QA's draft PASS verdict surfaced 3 real issues that QA missed on first pass: (1) stale '100 lines / line 61 / 56-62 / 136-141' references still present in BA-iter-2 spec text — QA's 'no surviving stale references' claim was demonstrably false; (2) context JSON AC8 (line 41) and requirement.what (line 10) still use unconditional 'only chrome-debug-profile removed' wording that contradicts ticket AC8 CASE 7a/7b conditional — QA's '12 ACs mirrored byte-for-byte' claim was materially false; (3) context line 317 corrupts the 'ui-specialist-chrome-*' OR-clause literal by dropping the asterisk, threatening AC8 grep verification. QA verified all 3 codex findings independently via direct sed -n line-reads of the cited lines.

summary: BA-iter-2 substantively addresses the 9 prior objections (OBJ-1..OBJ-9) and 3 of the 4 fresh-codex findings. However, fresh-codex Finding 2 ('Remove or update all stale 100 lines / line 61 / 56-62 / 136-141 claims in both markdown and JSON') was only PARTIALLY applied — 5 stale-state assertions survive in ticket (lines 29, 53, 207) and context (lines 138, 271-272). Additionally, context JSON AC8 (line 41) and requirement.what (line 10) still use the iteration-1 unconditional removal wording that directly contradicts ticket AC8's CASE 7a/7b conditional acceptance. Context line 317 contains a literal typo dropping the trailing asterisk on 'ui-specialist-chrome-*'. Verdict: FAIL with 3 small BA-iter-3 amendment requirements (OBJ-10/OBJ-11/OBJ-12).

objections.claim: QA-iter-2 report initially claimed 'no surviving stale references' to '100 lines / line 61 / 56-62 / 136-141', citing only ticket:260 (Technical Hints) and ticket:136 (OOS observation) as explicit retraction sites. BA-iter-2 codex Finding 2 PROPOSED_FIX explicitly required 'Remove or update all stale 100 lines / line 61 / 56-62 / 136-141 claims in both markdown and JSON' (verbatim from /var/tmp/codex-outputs/codex-output-ba-iter2-1779220500.txt).
objections.problem: Stale state assertions still present at: ticket:29 ('Current tmp-cleanup.sh line 61 matches chrome-debug-profile' — asserts a current state that no longer holds); ticket:53 ('/usr/local/sbin/tmp-cleanup.sh (existing daily cleanup, lines 56-62)' — stale line range); ticket:207 (AC7 step 5 OPTIONAL belt-and-braces check references 'lines 136-141 of the current script' — stale; live block is 152-157); context:138 (root_cause_analysis.localization_evidence asserts 'tmp-cleanup.sh exists at /usr/local/sbin/tmp-cleanup.sh with 100 lines; find expression at lines 56-61 with chrome-debug-profile on line 61'); context:271-272 (evidence.measured.value and .location both cite 'tmp-cleanup.sh line 61 has chrome-debug-profile clause'). Collectively these contradict the explicit retraction at ticket:260 and the conditional R7 CASE 7a/7b semantics. A dev reading from any of those sites could implement the wrong R7 case.
objections.required_evidence: BA must amend all 5 stale-reference sites in iteration 3: (1) ticket:29 — rewrite to describe earlier deployments historically, not as the current state; (2) ticket:53 — update Reference Source Location to '261 lines as of iteration-2 close; EXCLUDE_PRUNE at line 69; Playwright run_tmp block at lines 152-157'; (3) ticket:207 — AC7 step 5 must locate the find expression by HEADER ('--- Playwright profiles >1d in /tmp ---'), not by stale line range; (4) context:138 — rewrite localization_evidence to current 261-line/69/152-157 state; (5) context:271-272 — rewrite evidence.measured.value and .location to current 152-157-no-chrome-debug-profile-OR-chain + 69-EXCLUDE_PRUNE state.

objections.claim: QA-iter-2 report initially claimed '12 ACs mirrored byte-for-byte between ticket and context JSON success_criteria'. Spot-check confirms this is materially FALSE for AC8.
objections.problem: context:41 (success_criteria[7]) reads 'AC8: tmp-cleanup.sh preserves all other OR-clauses; only chrome-debug-profile removed.' — this is the iteration-1 UNCONDITIONAL removal wording. The ticket AC8 at lines 209-212 has been amended to CONDITIONAL CASE 7a/7b semantics: 'GIVEN the post-cycle /usr/local/sbin/tmp-cleanup.sh (whether dev took CASE 7a or CASE 7b) ... /tmp/chrome-debug-profile is protected from rm by either (a) absence from the find OR-chain (CASE 7a + EXCLUDE_PRUNE) or (b) presence in EXCLUDE_PRUNE (CASE 7b).' Additionally context:10 (requirement.what) still reads 'remove one find clause from cron cleanup script' — also unconditional. If dev reads context AC8 or requirement.what and follows the unconditional wording, dev will edit the live file (CASE 7b — already protected by EXCLUDE_PRUNE) UNNECESSARILY, creating a no-op edit that still mutates /usr/local/sbin/tmp-cleanup.sh against R7's CASE 7b 'make no changes' directive (ticket:80).
objections.required_evidence: BA must amend both context references in iteration 3: (1) context:41 — replace AC8 with: 'AC8: tmp-cleanup.sh preserves all four OR-clauses (happy-cdp-*, happy-live-rerun-cdp-*, happy-p05-chrome-*, ui-specialist-chrome-*) in the Playwright run_tmp block AND no chrome-debug-profile OR-clause inside that block; /tmp/chrome-debug-profile is protected from rm by either (a) absence from the OR-chain combined with EXCLUDE_PRUNE (CASE 7a post-edit), OR (b) EXCLUDE_PRUNE alone (CASE 7b documented no-op). Evaluated against whichever case dev took for R7.'; (2) context:10 — replace requirement.what with: 'Add lock + stale-page cleanup to wrapper CDP branch; CONDITIONALLY remove one find clause from cron cleanup script (CASE 7a) or document no-op (CASE 7b) based on live-file state at edit-time.'

objections.claim: Context JSON line 317 (context.file_contents[/usr/local/sbin/tmp-cleanup.sh]) describes the Playwright OR-chain as containing 'ui-specialist-chrome-' (no trailing asterisk).
objections.problem: Live file at /usr/local/sbin/tmp-cleanup.sh:157 (verified by QA Read) contains '-name ui-specialist-chrome-*' WITH the asterisk. AC8's verification recipe requires grepping for the literal 'ui-specialist-chrome-*' (ticket:212). The corrupted literal in context:317 risks dev or QA accepting a no-asterisk match that does NOT correspond to the actual file content, undermining the AC8 grep-for-exact-literals verification chain.
objections.required_evidence: BA must correct context:317 in iteration 3 to restore the trailing asterisk: '... happy-cdp-*, happy-live-rerun-cdp-*, happy-p05-chrome-*, ui-specialist-chrome-* (NO chrome-debug-profile OR-clause); closing paren at end of line 157.'

obj_1_through_obj_9.OBJ-1 (AC3 disposable endpoint): RESOLVED — ticket:172-175 (AC3) reads 'invoked with a DISPOSABLE unreachable --cdp-endpoint (e.g., http://127.0.0.1:9 or any other unbound TCP port verified empty via ss -lnt | grep -c :9 returning 0)'. Context JSON line 36 (success_criteria[2]) mirrors. Decouples from chrome-debug.service entirely, satisfying AC10 no-restart.
obj_1_through_obj_9.OBJ-2 (AC1 process-tree snapshot): RESOLVED — ticket:161-165 (AC1) requires 'pgrep/process-tree snapshots (recommended cadence: every 5 s while the second wrapper is waiting; minimum a single snapshot taken in the last 5 s before the second wrapper exits 75)' with the explicit assertion 'no /usr/bin/playwright-mcp process and no node child with parent-PID equal to the SECOND-WRAPPER-PID exists at any sampled moment before the second wrapper exits 75'. Recipe captures SECOND_WRAPPER_PID via $! and samples with both pgrep -P and ps --ppid. 'Exit-75 alone is insufficient evidence' is explicit. Context JSON line 34 (success_criteria[0]) mirrors and line 339 validation_approach AC1 codifies the recipe.
obj_1_through_obj_9.OBJ-3 (AC5 CHILD-PID /proc cmdline): RESOLVED — ticket:183-186 (AC5) requires the recipe '(1) identify the wrapper PID; (2) identify the CHILD PID by listing the wrapper's direct descendants (pgrep -P) and filtering to entry whose comm is node or playwright-mcp (NOT bash); (3) read /proc/<child-pid>/cmdline (NUL-separated argv) via tr 0 to n; assert the sequence --config followed by /root/.claude/playwright-config.json appears as two consecutive tokens'. 'Plain ps -ef | grep --config is INSUFFICIENT' is explicit. Context JSON line 38 mirrors; validation_approach AC5 codifies.
obj_1_through_obj_9.OBJ-4 (AC9/AC10 cycle_start_epoch + base: RESOLVED — ticket:138-157 (AC Verification Preconditions section) defines cycle_start_epoch=2026-05-19T17:53:39Z and mandates QA-side capture of SVC_SHA256_BASELINE, SVC_ACTIVE_BASELINE BEFORE any dev artifacts land. ticket:214-222 (AC9) requires sha256 EQUALS SVC_SHA256_BASELINE; ticket:224-230 (AC10) requires ActiveEnterTimestamp unchanged or predates cycle_start_epoch. Context JSON lines 42-43 mirror; validation_approach codifies. All five baselines (STEALTH_SHA256_BASELINE, SVC_SHA256_BASELINE, SVC_ACTIVE_BASELINE, LEAKED_PWMCP_BASELINE, pre-cycle file snapshots) are now mandatory preconditions.
obj_1_through_obj_9.OBJ-5 (AC7 /var/log/tmp-cleanup.log bloc: RESOLVED — ticket:198-207 (AC7) has a 5-step concrete recipe: (1) LOG_BEFORE=$(wc -l < /var/log/tmp-cleanup.log); (2) run --dry-run; (3) tail -n +$((LOG_BEFORE + 1)) to extract new block; (4) within '--- Playwright profiles >1d in /tmp ---' to next '---' header section, grep -c '^find:' MUST be 0; (5) optional belt-and-braces standalone find-expression check. The script's exec >> $LOG 2>&1 stderr-swallowing is explicitly documented as the reason non-zero exit codes are unreliable. Context JSON line 40 mirrors; validation_approach AC7 codifies.
obj_1_through_obj_9.OBJ-6 (AC11 lock-open-failure path): RESOLVED — ticket:232-239 (AC11) covers the R3 no-fallthrough invariant with 3 simulation modes (PREFERRED unshare -m + ro bind-mount /run; FALLBACK ulimit -n 3 exhausted fd; undecidable if neither available, BLOCKS cycle, default-pass forbidden). Assertions: exit 75 within ~1s, stderr diagnostic mentions lock-open failure, no playwright-mcp/node child via pgrep -P and ps --ppid checks. Context JSON line 44 mirrors; validation_approach AC11 codifies.
obj_1_through_obj_9.OBJ-7 (AC12 anti-cheat for no-reaping): RESOLVED — ticket:241-254 (AC12) has a 6-step concrete recipe spanning BOTH /usr/local/bin/playwright-mcp-stealth and /usr/local/sbin/tmp-cleanup.sh against pre-cycle snapshots (/tmp/pre-cycle.playwright-mcp-stealth.snapshot and /tmp/pre-cycle.tmp-cleanup.sh.snapshot). Includes (a) same-line grep, (b) multi-line PID-capture-then-signal pattern review, (c) pgrep -c invariance check (pre-edit == post-deploy-pre-first-new-invocation). Context JSON line 45 mirrors; validation_approach AC12 codifies.
obj_1_through_obj_9.OBJ-8 (AC4 transient-closure caveat): RESOLVED — ticket:177-181 (AC4) has the NOTE: 'AC4 validates a NEW post-launch session; preservation of pre-existing non-system page targets (e.g., the currently-active localhost:8765 Travel Plan tab) is explicitly NOT required and may be transiently closed by the cleanup phase on first invocation. This is the user-accepted 就地部署 impact.' Context JSON line 37 mirrors.
obj_1_through_obj_9.OBJ-9 (AC6 sha256 baseline + line-range): RESOLVED — ticket:188-196 (AC6) requires baseline capture via sha256sum < <(sed -n '104,130p' ...) on pre-edit lines 104-130, with line-shift handling (dev MUST document post-edit NEW_ELSE_START..NEW_ELSE_END). Precondition section ticket:142-144 makes baseline capture mandatory. Context JSON line 39 mirrors; validation_approach AC6 codifies.

fresh_codex_findings_1_to_4.Finding 1 (AC3 endpoint argv-driven not: RESOLVED — ticket:10 (Goal) reads 'cleanup ... against the wrapper's resolved --cdp-endpoint URL (normally http://127.0.0.1:8080 in production / in-place deployment, but resolved from argv — NOT hardcoded — so AC3 can exercise the no-Chrome path with a disposable unreachable endpoint such as http://127.0.0.1:9)'. ticket:32 (Evidence Expected) reads 'Cleanup phase POSTs to ${CDP_URL}/json/close/<id> (where CDP_URL is the wrapper's resolved --cdp-endpoint argv value, normally http://127.0.0.1:8080 in production)'. ticket:70 (R4) reads 'Resolve CDP_URL from the --cdp-endpoint argv value passed to this wrapper invocation. In production / in-place deployment this URL is normally http://127.0.0.1:8080. Cleanup probes use this resolved URL (NOT a hardcoded string).' Context JSON line 9 (clarified), line 275 (evidence.expected.value), line 333 (development_approach.strategy) all repeat 'argv-driven, NOT hardcoded 8080'. Remaining 8080 mentions in context JSON (lines 126, 134, 155, 318, 329) are either chrome-debug.service config descriptions OR the R7 verbatim source phrase — all correctly disambiguated. No drift.
fresh_codex_findings_1_to_4.Finding 2 (R7/AC8 CONDITIONAL CASE 7a/7b: PARTIALLY RESOLVED — the CASE 7a/7b conditional logic IS encoded (ticket:78-81, :113, :209-212; context:103 in_user_path:true), and ticket:260 + ticket:136 explicitly retract older line numbers in those specific blocks. HOWEVER, Finding 2 PROPOSED_FIX item 3 ('Remove or update all stale 100 lines / line 61 / 56-62 / 136-141 claims in both markdown and JSON') is only partially applied: 5 surviving stale-reference sites at ticket:29, :53, :207 and context:138, :271-272 — see OBJ-10. Additionally, Finding 2's mandate that the spec be self-consistent (no internal contradiction between R7 conditional and AC8 unconditional) is violated: context AC8 (context:41) and context requirement.what (context:10) retain iteration-1 unconditional removal wording — see OBJ-11.
fresh_codex_findings_1_to_4.Finding 3 (AC11 external simulation only: RESOLVED — ticket:233 (AC11 preamble) reads 'Important — locked-design constraint: the wrapper's lock path is always the single global /run/playwright-mcp.lock. Dev MUST NOT add a lock-path env-var/argv override hook to the wrapper for testing purposes — exposing such an override would weaken the single-global-lock invariant. Lock-open-failure must therefore be simulated EXTERNALLY (the wrapper's actual code path remains untouched while the simulation forces the open syscall to fail).' The two simulation modes (PREFERRED unshare; FALLBACK ulimit -n 3) are both external; the legacy iter-1 AC11 mode '(a) env-var/argv override' has been REMOVED. Context JSON line 44 reads 'Lock-path env-var/argv override is FORBIDDEN — weakens single-global-lock invariant'; validation_approach line 339 also reads 'DO NOT add a lock-path env-var/argv override to the wrapper for testing — that weakens the single-global-lock invariant'. Undecidable-rather-than-default-pass is explicit. NOTE: BA expanded codex's recipe slightly by adding ulimit -n 3 as a FALLBACK before undecidable, which codex did not explicitly enumerate. This is a stricter superset (more legitimate options before undecidable) and does not weaken the constraint; the spec text preserves both 'FORBIDDEN' for argv override AND 'undecidable if neither available' for the namespace+fd-exhaustion failure. Acceptable.
fresh_codex_findings_1_to_4.Finding 4 (AC12 multi-line PID-capture-t: RESOLVED — ticket:243-253 (AC12) now lists THREE pattern categories (same-line, multi-line, signal-sending built-ins) with a 6-step concrete recipe. Step 4 same-line grep. Step 5 explicit multi-line review: 'read EVERY full added hunk ... If any added hunk contains both a PID-capture pattern (pgrep -f playwright-mcp, pidof, ps ... grep playwright-mcp) AND any subsequent signal-sending verb on the captured variable (within the same hunk OR within a later hunk that obviously references the same variable name), that is an AC12 FAIL.' Both exact snapshot paths /tmp/pre-cycle.playwright-mcp-stealth.snapshot and /tmp/pre-cycle.tmp-cleanup.sh.snapshot are cited (steps 1-2). Context JSON line 45 mirrors; validation_approach AC12 codifies.

Finding 2 (in_scope_real_bug): cron Part B has contradictory current-file state, 261 lines / 152-157 not 246/136-141 — PROPOSED_FIX: make R7/AC8 conditional, mark OOS as in-user-path, REMOVE OR UPDATE ALL stale claims in markdown AND JSON. qa_codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex (gpt-5.5, xhigh) adversarially reviewed QA's draft PASS verdict and returned 3 in_scope_real_bug findings (1 = stale references partial closure of BA-iter-2 Finding 2; 2 = context AC8/requirement.what unconditional wording contradicts ticket AC8 CASE 7a/7b; 3 = context:317 literal corruption 'ui-specialist-chrome-' missing trailing asterisk) + 1 OBSERVATION_ONLY (AC11 ulimit fallback is acceptable stricter superset). QA verified all 3 in-scope findings independently via direct sed -n line-reads.
qa_codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: All 3 in-scope codex findings translated 1:1 into objections OBJ-10/OBJ-11/OBJ-12. Verdict downgraded from draft PASS to FAIL. OBSERVATION_ONLY (ulimit fallback) accepted as-is.

self_verification.evidence_notes: After codex adversarial review surfaced 3 missed issues, QA independently re-verified each by direct sed -n line-read of the cited ticket and context lines. Each codex finding was confirmed: ticket:29/53/207 each contain stale assertions; context:138 has the verbatim '100 lines / line 61' claim; context:271-272 cites 'line 61 has chrome-debug-profile'; context:41 has unconditional AC8; context:10 has unconditional requirement.what; context:317 has the asterisk-dropped 'ui-specialist-chrome-' literal. None of the codex findings was speculative — all 3 are independently reproducible by reading the cited file:line.
self_verification.initial_draft_self_verification_error: QA's initial draft self-verification claimed 'no surviving stale references' and '12 ACs mirrored byte-for-byte' — both claims were false. Lesson: token-overlap grep alone is not sufficient for cross-artifact consistency. Required: (a) explicit ticket-vs-context line-by-line AC-text equality check, (b) grep-for-stale-tokens audit (100 lines, line 61, 56-62, 136-141) across BOTH files, (c) sanity-check literal-preservation on every cited code fragment. QA should have run these before claiming PASS.

verdict_rationale: FAIL because BA-iter-2 only PARTIALLY closed fresh-codex Finding 2 (stale-line-number cleanup): the CASE 7a/7b conditional logic IS encoded correctly in ticket R7/AC8 (lines 78-81, 113, 209-212), but 5 stale-state assertions still survive in the spec text (ticket:29, :53, :207; context:138, :271-272). Context AC8 (context:41) and requirement.what (context:10) retain iteration-1 UNCONDITIONAL removal wording that contradicts the ticket's conditional. Context:317 corrupts the 'ui-specialist-chrome-*' literal by dropping the trailing asterisk. A dev reading from any surviving stale-state site OR from the unconditional context AC8/requirement.what could implement the wrong R7 case (e.g., unnecessary edit to the live file in CASE 7b state, against R7's no-op directive at ticket:80). BA must apply OBJ-10/OBJ-11/OBJ-12 in iteration 3. NOTE: The 9 prior objections (OBJ-1..OBJ-9) and 3 of 4 fresh-codex findings (1, 3, 4) ARE closed correctly. The 7 locked-design invariants are all preserved. This is a documentation-consistency failure, not a design failure — iteration 3 amendments should be small, focused, and mechanical.

iteration_note: This is iteration 2 of max 3 BA-QA cycles. Verdict FAIL → next BA dispatch becomes iteration 3 (last allowed). Required iteration-3 amendments are small and mechanical: (a) 5 stale-reference rewrites at ticket:29/:53/:207 and context:138/:271-272; (b) 2 context fields aligned to ticket AC8 CASE 7a/7b — context:10 (requirement.what) and context:41 (AC8); (c) 1 literal asterisk restoration at context:317. The 12 ticket ACs and 7 locked-design invariants are correct in the ticket markdown; only spec text around the ACs and the context JSON success_criteria/requirement.what need finishing touches.
